event: The Preaching of John Knox before the Lords of the Congregation
symbols: preacher in elevated pulpit — the centrality of the preached Word in Reformed theology
attentive congregation — the people hearing Scripture proclaimed
description: A dark, crowded interior of a church or assembly hall. At center-right, a figure in dark robes (John Knox) stands in an elevated pulpit, gesturing emphatically while preaching. Below and around him, a dense crowd of men and women fills the space — some seated, some standing, many in period dress of the mid-16th century. The lighting is warm and dramatic, with golden tones illuminating select faces and the preacher. The composition is densely packed and atmospheric, rendered in rich browns, golds, and deep shadows.

_meta.title: The Preaching of John Knox before the Lords of Congregation, 10 June 1559
